purpose: Recipe-B QLoRA SFT on gemma-4-E4B-it against the assembled sft-final dataset

host.gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (32607 MiB, Blackwell sm_120)

env.kind: Unsloth Studio venv (installed via https://unsloth.ai/install.sh, UNSLOTH_NO_TORCH=1, UNSLOTH_PYTHON=3.13)

divergences_from_installer_defaults: Installer's CUDA auto-detect maps driver CUDA 13.2 to the cu130 torch index (spec-forbidden); bypassed with UNSLOTH_NO_TORCH=1 and torch==2.11.0+cu129 pinned manually from https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu129

PyPI unsloth 2026.7.1 metadata declares torch<2.11.0 and transformers<=5.5.0; spec pins exceed both. Runtime-validated (CPU-only import + AutoConfig) despite declared bounds. uv pip check reports these as expected incompatibilities.

Studio single-env constraints pin transformers==4.57.6; base venv upgraded to 5.5.4 for model_type=gemma4 support (Studio's own vendored overlays at .venv_t5_530/.venv_t5_550/.venv_t5_510 = 5.3.0/5.5.0/5.10.2 are untouched).
xformers not installed: no cu129 cp313 wheel exists on the PyTorch index; sdpa is the mandated attention path anyway (spec 6.2).
torchao 0.17.0 + msgspec installed manually (Studio's overrides step self-skips in no-torch mode; 0.17.0 is Studio's own mapping for torch 2.11.x).

llama.cpp GGUF engine NOT installed (prebuilt fetch and source clone both failed on transient network timeouts; Studio reports 'limited: llama.cpp unavailable'). Only affects the later GGUF export/serving path (spec 6.3), not training. Retry 'unsloth studio' setup or build llama.cpp when network is stable; libcurl4-openssl-dev also missing for source builds.

preflight.status: all gates pass in CLI smoke mode

predownloaded_no_weights.unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it: config+tokenizer+chat_template cached; offline reload verified; model_type=gemma4, vocab 262144

env_snapshot.gpu_idle_compliance: No CUDA context created by any validation step (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES masked; torch.cuda.is_initialized()==False asserted at start and end; full 'import unsloth' deferred to GPU smoke because its _gpu_init probes the device by design)
